title: carex depressa var. subumbellata
intro: Carex depressa var. subumbellata, also known as the flat-stemmed sedge, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of flat-stemmed sedge include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce inflammation, and aid in the relief of congestion. Flat-stemmed sedge is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, where it is typically combined with other herbs like thyme and lemon balm to enhance its medicinal properties.
